bucket_summary.description: Isolated login failures are being read as repeated password guessing.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model treats failed-login vocabulary as sufficient evidence for brute force even when the log explicitly shows a single event.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Does the model ignore low counts such as failed_attempts=1 or count=1?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add brute-force anti-gravity normal examples with failed_attempts=1, count=1, isolated 4625, and paired burst examples where the count crosses the threshold.
bucket_summary.title: Normal -> brute force

bucket_summary.description: SQL injection payloads in login/API contexts are being pulled into auth failure logic.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model notices login/API/status cues before the SQL payload, especially when the request is blocked with 401, 403, 400, or 500-like status signals.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Does status/auth context dominate the SQL payload?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add SQLi positives inside login/API/search contexts with quote tautologies, UNION SELECT, SLEEP, stacked queries, and comment markers, including blocked status codes.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> brute force

bucket_summary.description: SQL quote/comment syntax is being confused with file/path attack syntax.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model treats SQL comment markers and quoted values as a generic injection shape, then selects directory traversal instead of the SQL-specific label.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Are quote or comment markers being mistaken for path traversal clues?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add paired examples contrasting SQL comments such as admin'-- with traversal paths such as ../../etc/passwd and encoded ../ sequences.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> traversal

bucket_summary.description: SQL vocabulary or OR patterns are not always strong enough to hold the SQLi label.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model sometimes treats information_schema or OR payloads as benign search content or generic probing unless the attack boundary is very explicit.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Is the payload too weak or ambiguous without a clearer SQL boundary?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add clearer SQLi positives with encoded tautologies, schema discovery in request parameters, and hard negatives where select/union/sleep/information_schema are benign text.
bucket_summary.title: SQLi -> normal/port

bucket_summary.description: Directory traversal examples are spread across normal, SQLi, port scan, and brute force.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: Traversal is no longer the primary blocker, but several traversal cases are still being overruled by status, user-agent, encoding, or generic suspicious cues.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Which non-path cue is distracting from ../, encoded traversal, or sensitive file access?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add traversal positives that keep the sensitive path token prominent, plus hard negatives where ../ appears in benign normalized documentation paths.
bucket_summary.title: Traversal boundary drift

bucket_summary.description: Reconnaissance signals are being pulled into the over-strong brute-force label.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model still uses generic suspicious wording as a shortcut for brute force, even when the evidence is clearly network reconnaissance.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Do blocked/attempt/enumeration cues overpower nmap, service enumeration, or port lists?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add port/recon positives with nmap fingerprint, service enumeration, unique_ports>=10, horizontal scans, and many hosts/ports, paired with auth-failure negatives.
bucket_summary.title: Port/recon -> brute force

bucket_summary.description: Horizontal scan evidence is not always enough to beat benign monitoring priors.
bucket_summary.diagnosis: The model underweights horizontal scan and unique host thresholds when the log lacks strong malicious wording.
bucket_summary.inspection_question: Does the model need a clearer threshold for hosts, ports, or scan window?
bucket_summary.suggested_repair_pattern: Add contrast pairs for inventory/health checks versus horizontal scan, using explicit unique_hosts and unique_ports thresholds.
bucket_summary.title: Port/recon -> normal

dataset_implications.details: Use login/API/search contexts with explicit payloads: quote tautology, UNION SELECT, SLEEP, information_schema, SQL comment markers, encoded SQLi, and stacked query syntax.
Use select, union, sleep, schema, and information_schema in documentation, search, or admin contexts without attack payload boundaries.
Use nmap fingerprint, SYN scan detected, service enumeration, horizontal scan, unique_ports>=10, unique_hosts>=10, and short scan windows.
Use authorized inventory, one-port monitoring, health checks, known scanner allowlists, and low unique port counts.
Use failed, blocked, 401, 403, 4625, or attempt wording without repeated login burst; pair failed_attempts=1 with failed_attempts>=10.

failures.diagnosis: The model treats failed-login vocabulary as sufficient evidence for brute force even when the log explicitly shows a single event.
failures.input_log: May 18 09:00:01 auth-01 sshd[2011]: Failed password for webapp from 192.0.2.44 port 51422 ssh2; failed_attempts=1
failures.inspection_question: Does the model ignore low counts such as failed_attempts=1 or count=1?
failures.predicted_reason: The SSH device repeated failed authentication attempts.
failures.suggested_repair_pattern: Add brute-force anti-gravity normal examples with failed_attempts=1, count=1, isolated 4625, and paired burst examples where the count crosses the threshold.

failures.diagnosis: The model notices login/API/status cues before the SQL payload, especially when the request is blocked with 401, 403, 400, or 500-like status signals.

failures.predicted_reason: The login request contains repeated POST requests with a single user name.
failures.suggested_repair_pattern: Add SQLi positives inside login/API/search contexts with quote tautologies, UNION SELECT, SLEEP, stacked queries, and comment markers, including blocked status codes.

hold_fixed_test: data/splits/test.jsonl remains held out and must not be used for selecting repair cases or tuning prompts/runtime settings.
next_step: Create the v3.4 boundary repair supplement from these buckets, then train and probe v3.4 on hard-contrast and mini semantic splits before any fixed test comparison.
purpose: Identify v3.3 hard-contrast label-boundary failures before creating the v3.4 boundary repair dataset.
